mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, designed for scalability and ease of use. It is ideal for teams looking to implement AI-driven applications in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.langgenius.com

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the Dify repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dify to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs from the Dify repository.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Dify into your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Dify to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow:
    name: example-workflow
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample Dify resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow:
    name: example-workflow
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dify installation and ensures that the Custom Resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get dify -n dify

kubectl describe dify example-dify -n dify

```

> This command lists the Dify resources in the specified namespace.
> This command provides detailed information about the created Dify resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dify from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls the Dify release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your resources. This step will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get dify -n dify -o yaml > dify-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get dify -n dify

```

> Backup the current Dify resource.
> Replace with the desired version if needed.
> Verify that the Dify resource is upgraded and running.
commands: kubectl get dify -n dify -o yaml > dify-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dify not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify installation may not have completed successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm installation logs and ensure the CRDs are applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Workflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified workflow in the Custom Resource does not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the workflow name in the Custom Resource matches an existing workflow.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server may be unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure your Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule Dify pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources available to your Kubernetes cluster or adjust the resource requests in the Dify configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agentic workflows and improved integration with external APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your existing workflows to use the new API endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify resources listed with 'STATUS: Running' when executing 'kubectl get dify -n dify'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow:
    name: example-workflow
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
